Title: Maize Se1 is a gene affecting sugary enhancer1 (se1) endosperm starch metabolism
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major carbohydrate reserve in cereal grain and important for human/animal nutrition and industrial use
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Wild-type Se1 promotes normal endosperm starch accumulation and suppresses excess soluble sugar/WSP accumulation; deletion of Se1 causes the se1 phenotype with lower starch and higher sucrose, maltose, and WSP, especially in su1-ref background
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: High-resolution map-based cloning; cosegregation of 637-bp deletion with phenotype; near-isogenic line comparison; metabolite profiling (GC-MS/UPLC-MS and carbohydrate assays); RNA-seq differential expression; RNAi knockdown of Se1 together with Su1 followed by RT-PCR and maltose assays
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly expressed in endosperm/developing kernel, peaking around 16-20 DAP; also detected in embryo and leaf; low in root and most other tissues
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in whole kernel and developing endosperm; absent in W822Gse deletion line; reduced in Se1-RNAi and Se1Su1-RNAi kernels
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Kernel developmental stage strongly affects phenotype and transcriptome; analyses focused on 11-21 DAP developing endosperm and 45 DAP mature kernels
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (NCBI BioProject PRJNA287265); untargeted metabolomics by GC-MS and UPLC-MS
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Maize ubiquitin 1 promoter plus enhancer intron 1 for RNAi hairpin; rice actin 1 promoter plus intron 1 enhancer for bar; Panicum virgatum ubiquitin 1 promoter for RFP
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Provides diagnostic marker for se1 allele and enables rapid introgression into elite sweet corn backgrounds to elevate sugar and modify starch traits; useful for sweet corn quality improvement, with attention to genetic-background dependence and interaction with su1
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Se1 is not a catalytic starch-pathway enzyme or TF; likely acts indirectly/regulatorily in starch metabolism. Deletion phenotype is strongest/validated in su1-ref background, and Se1 silencing alone did not produce obvious visible kernel phenotype in transgenics
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced starch accumulation and wrinkled/translucent kernels in se1 mutants; phenotype depends on su1 genetic background and may affect end-use quality/yield traits
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Fine mapping identified Se1 as Zm00001d007657 on chromosome 2, and a 637-bp deletion abolishing its coding region causes the se1 phenotype. 2. se1 mutant kernels in a su1-ref background show decreased starch and increased total sugar, WSP, sucrose, and maltose in endosperm. 3. Transcriptome data indicate pleiotropic effects of Se1 loss on both starch biosynthetic and starch degradation-associated genes. 4. Combined RNAi repression of Se1 and Su1 increased maltose beyond Su1-RNAi alone, validating Zm00001d007657 as causal for sugary enhancer1.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Starch is grain storage carbohydrate for nutrition and end-use quality
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduction of Su1 expression caused wrinkled kernels and increased maltose; reducing Se1 in the presence of reduced Su1 further increased maltose relative to Su1-RNAi alone
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi silencing of Su1 alone and combined Se1Su1-RNAi; semiquantitative RT-PCR; kernel phenotype scoring; maltose assay
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in endosperm; detectable in B73, W822GSe, and W822Gse kernels
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced in Su1-RNAi and Se1Su1-RNAi kernels
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Kernel development stage; 20-DAP kernels assayed in transgenic lines
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq differential expression in W822Gse vs W822GSe
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Maize ubiquitin 1 promoter plus enhancer intron 1 for RNAi hairpin
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important interacting locus to consider when deploying se1 for sweet corn quality; combined manipulation with Se1 can further increase maltose/sugary enhancer traits
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Su1 is a known starch enzyme but in this paper it serves as an experimentally co-manipulated interacting/background gene rather than the cloned primary target; se1 effects are interpreted largely in the su1-ref background
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing causes wrinkled kernels and altered starch accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Su1 knockdown alone was sufficient to produce wrinkled kernels. 2. Combined knockdown of Se1 and Su1 increased maltose more than Su1 knockdown alone. 3. This genetic interaction supports Se1 as a modifier of sugary1-dependent endosperm carbohydrate metabolism.
